opp.ais_why: Future Fund operates in a technology-driven investment environment where automation, data-driven decision-making, and operational efficiency are strategic priorities. Procurement organizations in similar public investment and financial institutions are increasingly evaluating AI for intake management, sourcing, contract lifecycle management, supplier risk, spend analytics, and autonomous procurement workflows. Given the broader adoption of AI across enterprise functions, Future Fund is likely to have a strong interest in AI-enabled procurement transformation, particularly if it aligns with governance, compliance, and productivity objectives. While there is no public confirmation of a specific AI procurement initiative, the organization's digital transformation focus indicates a favorable environment for AI-powered Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions.

champion.narrative: Chris Stevenson (Director, Procurement, Executive Sponsor per Salesforce contact role) is the only engaged stakeholder. He is championing the procurement transformation initiative and is actively evaluating vendors, but his championship is developing, not strong. Evidence of advocacy: Chris initiated the 7 Apr 2026 discovery call, described detailed requirements and workflows, and asked for a demo. However, Chris has not introduced Zycus to the CFO (the Economic Buyer) or the CTO (integration validation owner), has not provided internal documents or an RFP, has not pushed back on competitors in favor of Zycus, and is actively praising Omnia ('the future') in the same breath as evaluating Zycus. Chris's engagement is cooling: the demo was planned in the 19 May Next Step but is still not scheduled as of 1 Jul (6+ weeks later), and the last Salesforce activity is the rep's 1 Jul Next Step noting that the rep will reach out 31 Jul. Chris has access to the CFO (he reports directly to the CFO per his 7 Apr statement), but he has not facilitated that access for Zycus. Chris is a developing champion who could become strong if Zycus delivers a compelling demo, provides the Victorian government references and integration clarity he is asking for, and helps him build the business case to take to the CFO. However, if the demo stalls or another vendor (Omnia) delivers a stronger Teams integration demo and multi-threads to the CFO first, Chris could disengage or become a coach rather than a champion. Strengthening Chris's championship (deliver the demo, provide the references and integration clarity, co-create the business case, ask Chris to introduce Zycus to the CFO) is a near-term move.

competition.narrative: Future Fund is actively evaluating multiple alternatives in parallel: Omnia (the highest threat), Zip, SAP Ariba (listed in Salesforce Competitors__c), and build-in-house with Future Fund's own AI team. Chris Stevenson stated on the 7 Apr 2026 call that he is 'looking at Omnia, Zip, and a few others', and praised Omnia's Microsoft Teams integration demo, saying it is 'the future'. This makes Omnia the primary competitive threat, positioned as the Teams-native, conversational intake leader, which is exactly what Chris wants and a top decision criterion. Chris also asked 'do I pick something off the shelf or build internally with our own AI capability?', indicating that build-in-house is a serious alternative. Future Fund has a large, sophisticated in-house AI team building investment tools, and Chris is evaluating whether they can build intake orchestration + CLM + AI contract analysis internally rather than buy SaaS. Zycus's wedge to win: (1) Demonstrate that Merlin Intake delivers the same conversational Teams experience as Omnia, with deeper orchestration (the 3 workflows + compliance decision tree) that Omnia may not offer. (2) Show proven Victorian government deployments (panel assignment, indigenous supplier checks, audit trails) that de-risk the decision vs a greenfield internal build and prove Zycus can deliver out-of-the-box Commonwealth compliance workflows that would take Future Fund's AI team years to design, build, and certify. (3) Demonstrate clear time-to-value advantage: vendor selection 30 Jun 2027, go-live Jul 2027 (Chris's stated timeline) vs an 18-24 month internal build. (4) Position Merlin as the AI differentiation (contract analysis, spend intelligence, negotiation agent) that is beyond the scope of a basic intake tool, making the build-vs-buy trade-off favor Zycus. The competitive positioning must be activated in the demo and in the business case Chris takes to the CFO, or Omnia will win on Teams integration and the build-in-house alternative will delay or kill the deal.

decision_criteria.narrative: Chris Stevenson described detailed evaluation criteria on the 7 Apr 2026 call and in the 19 May and 1 Jul Next Steps. The criteria are: (1) End-user experience: must allow end-users to request and purchase through Microsoft Teams using conversational language (Merlin Intake agent), without learning a new portal. Chris praised Omnia's Teams demo as 'the future', making this a top criterion. (2) Compliance orchestration: must enforce Future Fund's decision tree and mandatory workflows: category lookup to mandatory panel, indigenous supplier check prompt with evidence capture, value-threshold-driven approval paths, evaluation panel with conflict-of-interest forms and attachments, and a single auditable record capturing all required fields. (3) Integration: must integrate with Workday (source spend/PO/contract data into Zycus for searchability), ServiceNow (replace or coexist for intake requests), DocuSign (eSignatures in CLM), and government tender portals (panel assignment, indigenous supplier checks, cost capture). (4) Proven government deployments: Chris asked for examples of Victorian government departments using Zycus with government tender portals, indicating he wants to see proven public-sector implementations to de-risk the decision. (5) Time-to-value vs build-in-house: Chris explicitly asked 'do I pick something off the shelf or build internally with our own AI capability?', indicating that speed to go-live (vendor selection 30 Jun 2027, live Jul 2027) vs an 18-24 month internal build is a criterion. These criteria are confirmed from Chris's own words and the Next Step action items, not inferred.

Build-vs-buy strategic threat: Chris questioned on 7 Apr call 'do I pick something off the shelf or build internally with our own AI capability?' Future Fund has large, sophisticated in-house AI team building investment tools; if Zycus cannot demonstrate clear time-to-value advantage (vendor selection 30 Jun 2027, live Jul 2027 per Chris's timeline vs 18-24 month internal build), out-of-the-box Commonwealth compliance workflows (panel/indigenous/audit) that would take years to design/build/certify, and proven Victorian gov deployments (lower risk than greenfield build), Chris may delay vendor selection, choose lighter SaaS and build AI in-house, or fully commit to internal build. This risk is compounded by weak AI positioning on 7 Apr call—Chris's takeaway was still 'can we build this?' not 'Zycus AI is clearly superior.'

Current close date 17 Sep 2026 (83 days out) is 9-10 months ahead of Chris Stevenson's stated buyer timeline. On 7 Apr call Chris said he aims to have technology providers 'lined up by 30 Jun [2027]' for implementation start 'Jul 2027,' implying vendor selection in mid-2027 and go-live in H2 2027 or early 2028—roughly 13-15 months from the 7 Apr call, not 5 months from today. The Sep 2026 close date is a placeholder and will slip. While this is factored into the verdict (Close Date Risk: healthy deal, optimistic date), it creates forecast and pipeline management risk: if leadership expects a Sep 2026 close and the deal does not move to validation/proposal by Aug 2026, there may be internal pressure to push the deal or down-grade forecast, neither of which reflects the buyer's actual timeline. Recommend: confirm the true vendor-selection and go-live dates in writing with Chris during the demo follow-up, update the Salesforce close date to align with Chris's stated timeline (likely mid-2027), and set internal expectations that this is a mid-2027 close, not Sep 2026.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Chris Stevenson's stated requirements align with Zycus's capabilities, but Zycus has not yet demonstrated the fit. Key alignments: (1) Conversational Teams intake (Merlin Intake agent), (2) Compliance orchestration (3 workflows + decision tree), (3) Integration (Workday, ServiceNow, DocuSign, gov tender portals), (4) Proven government deployments. However, Zycus has not yet delivered the demo, provided the Victorian government references, or clarified the integration approach, so the fit is still to be proven. Chris's praise of Omnia's Teams demo ('the future') and his build-vs-buy question indicate he is not yet convinced Zycus is the best fit.

hard.ais_why: Future Fund operates in a technology-driven investment environment where automation, data-driven decision-making, and operational efficiency are strategic priorities. Procurement organizations in similar public investment and financial institutions are increasingly evaluating AI for intake management, sourcing, contract lifecycle management, supplier risk, spend analytics, and autonomous procurement workflows. Given the broader adoption of AI across enterprise functions, Future Fund is likely to have a strong interest in AI-enabled procurement transformation, particularly if it aligns with governance, compliance, and productivity objectives. While there is no public confirmation of a specific AI procurement initiative, the organization's digital transformation focus indicates a favorable environment for AI-powered Source-to-Pay (S2P) solutions.

deltas.subject: Build-vs-buy strategic threat: Chris asked on 7 Apr call 'do I pick something off the shelf or build internally with our own AI capability?' Future Fund has large, sophisticated in-house AI team building investment tools. Chris is evaluating whether Future Fund's AI team can build intake orchestration + CLM + AI contract analysis internally rather than buy SaaS. If Zycus cannot demonstrate clear time-to-value advantage (vendor selection 30 Jun 2027, live Jul 2027 per Chris vs 18-24 month internal build), out-of-the-box Commonwealth compliance workflows (would take years for AI team to design/build/certify), and proven Victorian gov deployments (lower risk than greenfield build), Chris may delay vendor selection, choose lighter SaaS tool and build AI features in-house, or commit to internal build.

Integration requirements (Workday, ServiceNow, DocuSign, gov tender portals) raised by Chris on 7 Apr call but not yet answered. Chris asked how Merlin sources spend/PO/contract data from Workday into Zycus data core, whether Zycus replaces ServiceNow or coexists, and how DocuSign integrates for eSignatures. 19 May Next Step lists these as demo agenda items ('de-risk integrations early') but Zycus has not yet consulted solution consulting team or provided clarity (open Zycus deliverable). If integrations are more complex or costly than expected (e.g. Workday custom fields, ServiceNow replacement = change management, gov tender portal APIs unavailable), it could delay implementation, increase cost, or weaken the business case vs build-in-house. Addressing integration feasibility and effort in the demo and involving Zycus solution consulting + Future Fund's CTO/tech lead is a near-term move.

The Sep 2026 close date is a placeholder and will slip. Recommend: confirm true vendor-selection and go-live dates in writing with Chris during demo follow-up, update SF close date to align with Chris's stated timeline (likely mid-2027), and set internal expectations that this is a mid-2027 close, not Sep 2026.

deltas.subject: Competitive risk: Omnia praised for Teams demo ('the future'), positioning as Teams-native leader on a key criterion. Show how the system enforces the compliance decision tree: (1) Category → mandatory panel lookup, (2) Indigenous supplier check prompt + evidence capture, (3) Value thresholds → approval paths, (4) Evaluation panel + COI forms + attachments, (5) Single auditable record (what must be captured, where).
Clarify integration approach: how Merlin Intake sources spend/PO/contract data from the relevant stakeholder into Zycus data core for searchability and orchestration.
Explain ServiceNow decision: does Zycus replace ServiceNow for intake/requests, or do they coexist? Show the touchpoints.
Show DocuSign integration for eSignatures in the contracting/CLM workflow.
Provide examples of how Victorian government departments are using Zycus with government tender portals: panel assignment, indigenous supplier checks, cost capture, and relevant Victoria use-cases.

Single-threaded deal in a competitive multi-vendor evaluation (Zip, Omnia active, build-vs-buy open) with only Chris Stevenson (Director Procurement, Executive Sponsor) engaged and no access to CFO (budget authority, procurement reports to CFO), CTO (integration validation required for Workday/ServiceNow/DocuSign), or other C-level stakeholders. If another vendor (especially Omnia, the current highest threat based on Chris's 7 Apr praise of its Teams demo as 'the future') multi-threads to the CFO or CTO and gains executive sponsorship before Zycus does, Zycus loses control and visibility. Risk: ghost if Zycus remains single-threaded and another vendor builds C-level sponsorship.

Build-vs-buy strategic threat: Chris questioned on 7 Apr call 'do I pick something off the shelf or build internally with our own AI capability?' Future Fund has a large, sophisticated in-house AI team building investment tools, and Chris is evaluating whether Future Fund's AI team can build intake orchestration + CLM + AI contract analysis internally rather than buying SaaS. If Zycus cannot demonstrate clear time-to-value advantage (vendor selection 30 Jun 2027, live Jul 2027 per Chris's timeline vs 18-24 month internal build), out-of-the-box Commonwealth compliance workflows (panel/indigenous/audit) that would take years for Future Fund's AI team to design/build/certify, and proven Victorian gov deployments (lower risk than greenfield build), Chris may delay vendor selection, choose a lighter SaaS tool and build AI features in-house, or fully commit to internal build. This risk is compounded by Zycus's weak AI positioning on the 7 Apr call—Chris's takeaway was still 'can we build this?' not 'Zycus AI is clearly superior.'

Current close date 17 Sep 2026 (80 days out) is 9-10 months ahead of Chris Stevenson's stated buyer timeline. On 7 Apr call Chris said he aims to have technology providers 'lined up by 30 Jun [2027]' for implementation start 'Jul 2027,' implying vendor selection in mid-2027 and go-live in H2 2027 or early 2028—roughly 13-15 months from the 7 Apr call, not 5 months from today. The Sep 2026 close date is a placeholder and will slip. While this is factored into the verdict (Close Date Risk: healthy deal, optimistic date), it creates forecast and pipeline management risk: if leadership expects a Sep 2026 close and the deal does not move to validation/proposal by Aug 2026, there may be internal pressure to push the deal or down-grade forecast, neither of which reflects the buyer's actual timeline. Recommend: confirm the true vendor-selection and go-live dates in writing with Chris during the demo follow-up, update the Salesforce close date to align with Chris's stated timeline (likely mid-2027), and set internal expectations that this is a mid-2027 close, not Sep 2026.

Integration requirements (Workday, ServiceNow, DocuSign, gov tender portals) raised by Chris on 7 Apr call but not yet answered. Chris asked how Merlin sources spend/PO/contract data from Workday into Zycus data core, whether Zycus replaces ServiceNow or coexists, and how DocuSign integrates for eSignatures. 19 May Next Step lists these as demo agenda items ('de-risk integrations early') but Zycus has not yet consulted solution consulting team or provided clarity (open Zycus deliverable). If integrations are more complex or costly than expected (e.g. Workday custom fields, ServiceNow replacement = change management, gov tender portal APIs unavailable), it could delay implementation, increase cost, or weaken the business case vs build-in-house. Addressing integration feasibility and effort in the demo and involving Zycus solution consulting + Future Fund's CTO/tech lead is a near-term move.

Economic Buyer not engaged: Chris stated 'procurement reports into the CFO' on 7 Apr call, indicating CFO controls budget, but CFO has not been named or contacted. No commercial or pricing conversation has occurred. Mapping and engaging the CFO is critical to secure budget approval and executive sponsorship.

Demo follow-up stalled: Next Step from 19 May states 'following up on scheduling a demo' with detailed agenda (3 workflows, decision tree, integrations). 40+ days later (as of 29 Jun) demo not yet scheduled. Last SF activity 11 Jun (18 days ago). If follow-up continues to stall, Chris may disengage or prioritize another vendor (especially Omnia, who has already demo'd).

Competition active and Omnia ahead: Chris praised Omnia's Teams demo on 7 Apr call ('I saw them demo... that is almost the future'), indicating Omnia has already engaged, demo'd, and impressed Chris. Omnia is ahead on mindshare. Build-vs-buy also open (Chris: 'do I pick something off the shelf or build internally?'). Zycus has not yet delivered demo or proven AI superiority. Competitive positioning and demo delivery in next 14-30 days is critical.

No quantified business case: Chris described pain qualitatively on 7 Apr call but provided no quantified impact (headcount, cycle time, savings). Without quantified metrics, it is harder to justify $350k investment to CFO and defend budget vs build-vs-buy. Building the business case with Chris and presenting to CFO is a near-term move.

Deliver the tailored demo within 14 days to differentiate vs Omnia and validate integration feasibility. Demo follow-up has stalled 6+ weeks since 19 May Next_Step; Chris's most recent reference point is Omnia's Teams demo which he praised as 'the future.' If Zycus does not re-engage and deliver the demo soon, Omnia's positioning strengthens and Zycus loses momentum. value.detail: Current close date 17 Sep 2026 (83 days out) is 9-10 months ahead of Chris Stevenson's stated buyer timeline. On 7 Apr call Chris said he aims to have technology providers 'lined up by 30 Jun [2027]' for implementation start 'Jul 2027,' implying vendor selection in mid-2027 and go-live in H2 2027 or early 2028—roughly 13-15 months from the 7 Apr call, not 5 months from today. Recommend: confirm the true vendor-selection and go-live dates in writing with Chris during the demo follow-up, update the Salesforce close date to align with Chris's stated timeline (likely mid-2027), and set internal expectations that this is a mid-2027 close, not Sep 2026.

value.detail: Build-vs-buy strategic threat: Chris asked on 7 Apr call 'do I pick something off the shelf or build internally with our own AI capability?' Future Fund has a large, sophisticated in-house AI team building investment tools, and Chris is evaluating whether Future Fund's AI team can build intake orchestration + CLM + AI contract analysis internally rather than buy SaaS. If Zycus cannot demonstrate clear time-to-value advantage (vendor selection 30 Jun 2027, live Jul 2027 per Chris's timeline vs 18-24 month internal build), out-of-the-box Commonwealth compliance workflows (panel/indigenous/audit) that would take years for Future Fund's AI team to design/build/certify, and proven Victorian gov deployments (lower risk than greenfield build), Chris may delay vendor selection, choose a lighter SaaS tool and build AI features in-house, or fully commit to internal build. This risk is compounded by Zycus's weak AI positioning on the 7 Apr call—Chris's takeaway was still 'can we build this?' not 'Zycus AI is clearly superior.'
